items.title: Iran's Qalibaf Says Tehran Sees Through 'American Games' as Tensions Deepen
items.desc: Iranian Parliament Speaker Mohammed Baqer Qalibaf said Tehran judges Washington by its military actions rather than its rhetoric, as regional tensions persist and diplomatic contacts continue through mediators.

items.title: KRG Expands Gashanawa Project With 150 Million Dinar Support for SMEs
items.desc: The Kurdistan Regional Government will launch a new phase of Project Gashanawa before the end of 2026, providing up to 150 million Iraqi dinars for more than 100 eligible small and medium-sized enterprises through a fully digital application and evaluation system.

items.title: $10M+ Daquq Bridge reopens in Iraq’s Kirkuk
items.desc: Shafaq News- Kirkuk The Iraqi Ministry of Construction, Housing, and Public Municipalities opened the rebuilt Daquq Bridge in Kirkuk province on Monday, the provincial Roads and Bridges Director Ali Akbar told Shafaq News. Akbar said the one-kilometer-long, 12.5-meter-wide bridge had officially entered service on the “strategic” Baghdad-Kirkuk route, which connects northern Iraq with c

items.title: Yemen's Houthis declare naval blockade on Saudi Arabia
items.desc: Shafaq News- Sanaa Yemen's Houthi (Ansarallah) movement on Monday imposed a ban on maritime navigation involving Saudi Arabia, warning that any military escalation by Riyadh would trigger a "comprehensive and severe" response. Military spokesperson Yahya Saree presented the group's "blockade for blockade" strategy, linking the move to Saudi Arabia's continued "unjust and oppressive siege" on Yemen

items.title: Hamas selects Khalil Al-Hayya new Political Chief
items.desc: Shafaq News- Gaza Hamas has appointed senior official Khalil Al-Hayya as the new head of its political bureau, the group disclosed on Monday, succeeding Yahya Sinwar, who led the movement’s political leadership until his death in an Israeli strike in October 2024. According to a statement, the appointment follows an internal election process launched by Hamas in May to choose a new leader af

items.title: SOMO to revive Iraq’s gas oil purchases after 18 months
items.desc: Shafaq News- Baghdad Iraq’s State Oil Marketing Organization (SOMO) is seeking to buy 127,500 tons (949,875 barrels) of gas oil for August and September delivery, its first such purchase since January 2025, according to a tender document reviewed by Reuters. The state marketer requested gas oil containing 2,500 parts per million of sulfur, with the first 40,000-ton cargo to be loaded by Augu

items.title: Overworked Iraqi private school teachers demand reform
items.desc: Shafaq News- Baghdad Private schools have become a growing alternative for Iraqi families struggling with overcrowded public classrooms, but the teachers driving the sector’s expansion continue to face low wages, heavy workloads and limited employment protections. Thousands of educators have raised concerns that their wages have not matched the rapid growth of private schooling, with many ea

items.title: 3 U.S. personnel killed in Iran conflict. And, Hunter Biden opens up to NPR
items.desc: Three U.S. service members have been killed since Friday amid rising tension between the U.S. and Iran. And, Hunter Biden talks to NPR's Newsmakers about addiction and public scrutiny.

items.title: At least 930 people have died in Congo's Ebola outbreak
items.desc: Congo's health ministry said the deaths were recorded out of the 2,344 cases confirmed as of Saturday. Thirty-seven new deaths were recorded in the previous 24 hours in one of the highest totals.

items.title: U.S. and Iran launch new strikes after 3 U.S. service members are killed
items.desc: The U.S. attacks were the ninth consecutive day of bombing since peace talks between the two countries collapsed in dispute over a memorandum of understanding focused on the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Andy Burnham becomes U.K. prime minister
items.desc: In a transition of power presided over by King Charles, Andy Burnham has replaced Keir Starmer as the United Kingdom's prime minister. Both are from the center-left Labour Party.

items.title: Who is Andy Burnham, the U.K.'s new prime minister?
items.desc: On Monday, former Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham succeeds Keir Starmer as the United Kingdom's prime minister. Burnham's politics have been shaped by England's north-south class divide.
